message: br-bd-ye0f4: isolate tls dtor test capture

 {"id":"bd-yaiw","title":"Repair beads SQLite integrity from JSONL source of truth","description":"Repair the local Beads SQLite database after doctor reported a recoverable integrity warning while the JSONL source of truth remained healthy. Current state: issues.jsonl parses with 613 records, br sync status reports the DB and JSONL are in sync with dirty_count 0, but br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check warning database disk image is malformed page 17 is never used. Recovery plan: snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated temporary DB from issues.jsonl, verify the temporary DB with br show, br sync --status, and br doctor, then promote it to canonical .beads/beads.db without deleting any historical artifacts.","notes":"Repair summary: snapshot created at .beads/.br_recovery/20260408T032423Z_bd-yaiw. Direct temp rebuild via br sync --import-only --rebuild into .beads/beads.db.rebuild_20260408T032435Z preserved data but still reproduced the SQLite integrity warning. A second-stage VACUUM INTO from that rebuilt DB produced .beads/beads.db.vacuum_20260408T032435Z, which passed SQLite integrity_check, br show, br sync --status, and br doctor. Promotion was non-destructive: moved the prior canonical DB and sidecars to timestamped .bad files, then installed the verified vacuumed DB as .beads/beads.db. Post-promotion state: br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check ok; remaining warnings are preserved recovery artifacts and a wal-without-shm sidecar expected for frankensqlite.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:21:58.390640706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:05.440726927Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:05.440523393Z","close_reason":"Canonical .beads/beads.db repaired; final post-write vacuum pending to keep integrity_check clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","coordination","infrastructure"]}

-{"id":"bd-ye0f4","title":"[CRITICAL] bd-g2bd5 regression: glibc_internal_abi_test still SIGABRTs on free(): invalid size under parallel test execution","description":"## Regression against bd-g2bd5

bd-g2bd5 was closed at 2026-04-22T14:01:10Z with close_reason 'Scoped __cxa_thread_atexit_impl capture to local TLS dtor tests and revalidated glibc/startup ABI suites' (commit a581f0ac 'br-bd-g2bd5: isolate tls dtor test registrations'). That fix is partial — the heap corruption still reproduces.

## Fresh repro at current HEAD (901b7bcc / 693bb766)

```
$ rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test --workspace --all-targets
...
test e2e_string_pipeline_strlen_malloc_memcpy_strcmp ... ok
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
  process didn't exit successfully: `.../glibc_internal_abi_test-bcae966c1a14117c` (signal: 6, SIGABRT: process abort signal)
```

Same binary re-run in isolation (`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`) now appears to pass — so the race has moved from 'reliably reproducing on isolated run' (pre-fix) to 'only reproducing when other workspace tests run concurrently in the same process pool' (post-fix). That's measurable progress, but the heap write onto a freed-or-wrong-size chunk is still live.

## Diagnosis

The a581f0ac fix scoped  capture to its test-local TLS dtor registrations. That closed **one** source of cross-test thread-local leakage. The SIGABRT still firing near  (in a different test binary) suggests either:

1. another set of test TLS/thread-local state is leaking through the same path (e.g. resolv-context override, IDNA allocation pool, wcstof128 static, obstack globals), OR
2. the fix captured TLS dtor registrations correctly but freed its scratch buffer via a path that is inconsistent with the allocation size metadata (e.g. Box::from_raw on a pointer allocated by a different allocator, or layout mismatch on dealloc).

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with MALLOC_CHECK_=2 to get a glibc backtrace from the first write past chunk-size metadata.
2. Rerun under ASan (RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address' cargo +nightly test --workspace) — ASan will point at the exact offending free/realloc.
3. Audit remaining thread-local state init/teardown in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs plus any Box::from_raw / custom Drop impl that crosses allocators.

## Provenance / cross-reference

- Previous bead: bd-g2bd5 (closed 2026-04-22T14:01:10Z).
- Previous fix commit: a581f0ac br-bd-g2bd5: isolate tls dtor test registrations.
- Also closed this session: bd-i3om4 (70d3f3ea br-bd-i3om4: serialize runtime policy test state) and bd-nnjiz (901b7bcc br-bd-nnjiz: normalize elimination flake threshold). Both appear to hold under parallel reruns.

## Blast radius

Still blocks cargo test --workspace --all-targets from completing cleanly. --test-threads=1 is a workaround, not a fix.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:21:38.932129482Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:21:38.932129482Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","critical","full-review","glibc","heap","race","regression","test"]}
+{"id":"bd-ye0f4","title":"[CRITICAL] bd-g2bd5 regression: glibc_internal_abi_test still SIGABRTs on free(): invalid size under parallel test execution","description":"## Regression against bd-g2bd5

bd-g2bd5 was closed at 2026-04-22T14:01:10Z with close_reason 'Scoped __cxa_thread_atexit_impl capture to local TLS dtor tests and revalidated glibc/startup ABI suites' (commit a581f0ac 'br-bd-g2bd5: isolate tls dtor test registrations'). That fix is partial — the heap corruption still reproduces.

## Fresh repro at current HEAD (901b7bcc / 693bb766)

```
$ rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test --workspace --all-targets
...
test e2e_string_pipeline_strlen_malloc_memcpy_strcmp ... ok
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
  process didn't exit successfully: `.../glibc_internal_abi_test-bcae966c1a14117c` (signal: 6, SIGABRT: process abort signal)
```

Same binary re-run in isolation (`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`) now appears to pass — so the race has moved from 'reliably reproducing on isolated run' (pre-fix) to 'only reproducing when other workspace tests run concurrently in the same process pool' (post-fix). That's measurable progress, but the heap write onto a freed-or-wrong-size chunk is still live.

## Diagnosis

The a581f0ac fix scoped  capture to its test-local TLS dtor registrations. That closed **one** source of cross-test thread-local leakage. The SIGABRT still firing near  (in a different test binary) suggests either:

1. another set of test TLS/thread-local state is leaking through the same path (e.g. resolv-context override, IDNA allocation pool, wcstof128 static, obstack globals), OR
2. the fix captured TLS dtor registrations correctly but freed its scratch buffer via a path that is inconsistent with the allocation size metadata (e.g. Box::from_raw on a pointer allocated by a different allocator, or layout mismatch on dealloc).

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with MALLOC_CHECK_=2 to get a glibc backtrace from the first write past chunk-size metadata.
2. Rerun under ASan (RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address' cargo +nightly test --workspace) — ASan will point at the exact offending free/realloc.
3. Audit remaining thread-local state init/teardown in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs plus any Box::from_raw / custom Drop impl that crosses allocators.

## Provenance / cross-reference

- Previous bead: bd-g2bd5 (closed 2026-04-22T14:01:10Z).
- Previous fix commit: a581f0ac br-bd-g2bd5: isolate tls dtor test registrations.
- Also closed this session: bd-i3om4 (70d3f3ea br-bd-i3om4: serialize runtime policy test state) and bd-nnjiz (901b7bcc br-bd-nnjiz: normalize elimination flake threshold). Both appear to hold under parallel reruns.

## Blast radius

Still blocks cargo test --workspace --all-targets from completing cleanly. --test-threads=1 is a workaround, not a fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:21:38.932129482Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:50:08.906939824Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:50:08.905039477Z","close_reason":"Fixed TLS-dtor test capture isolation; workspace now advances past glibc_internal_abi_test to pthread_abi_test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","critical","full-review","glibc","heap","race","regression","test"]}
